title: _run_state.json -- pipeline run-state and backup tracker v0.2
description: The single source of truth for where a run stopped -- see harness-guardrails P7 and P10. Every stage's status is recorded here with timestamps; a crash or resume reads this file (via the DAO's get_last_passed_stage query) instead of re-deriving progress from scattered output files. After every stage passes, the orchestrator takes a full cumulative snapshot and records its path here -- one snapshot per passed stage, kept for the life of the run, never overwritten. v0.2: stage_name is an enum of the canonical stage names -- CASE_021's first full end-to-end run showed that free-form names silently fork one stage into several entries (document_processing vs document-pipeline, critic vs critic_v1) whenever a tool and an agent pick different spellings, which breaks get_last_passed_stage's resume logic and every consumer keying on stage_name.

human_input_status.description: Tracks P7's human-input waits. Entries are never deleted, only updated in place (waiting -> received), so the full history of what was waited on stays visible.

stage_name.description: Canonical stage names -- the ONLY values any producer (tool or agent) may write. Snake_case, versioned where the pipeline genuinely runs the stage once per draft version. If a new stage is added to the pipeline, it gets added here in the same change (D4).

attempt_count.description: Fixed retries under P9 -- max 3 for a partial/failed stage before it halts for user audit.

backup_path.description: outputs/CASE_XXX/_backups/step_<N>_<stage_name>/ -- null until this stage has passed.

description.description: What input is/was pending -- e.g. 'expert review of draft_report_v1'.
